events.text: Patient is a 38-year-old African American female presenting with a pigmented lesion on her right forearm that has been growing over the past 6 months.
At age 38, patient was diagnosed with superficial spreading melanoma (ICD-O morphology 8743/3) of the skin of the upper limb (ICD-O topography C44.6), staged as Stage IIIA (T2bN1aM0).

At age 38, shave biopsy followed by excisional biopsy of the right forearm lesion revealed superficial spreading melanoma with Breslow thickness of 1.8 mm, Clark level IV, ulceration present, mitotic rate 3/mm2, no regression, no microsatellites, and negative deep margin on excision.
At age 38, wide local excision with 1-2 cm margins of the right forearm melanoma was performed with primary closure.
At age 38, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right axilla identified 1 of 2 sentinel nodes positive for micrometastatic melanoma deposit measuring 0.4 mm detected by immunohistochemistry only.
At age 38, sentinel lymph node pathology showed isolated tumor cells to micrometastasis (0.4 mm) in 1 of 2 nodes, S100 and Melan-A positive, no extracapsular extension.

At age 38, next-generation sequencing of the primary melanoma identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 35%), NRAS wild-type, no TERT promoter mutation, wild-type TP53, and tumor mutational burden of 8 mutations per megabase.
At age 38, baseline PET/CT showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease.
At age 38, MRI brain with gadolinium contrast was negative for intracranial metastases.
At age 38, LDH was normal at 145 U/L, and multidisciplinary tumor board recommended adjuvant systemic therapy given sentinel node positivity.
At age 38, adjuvant dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for resected Stage IIIA BRAF V600E melanoma per COMBI-AD trial data.

At age 38, patient developed grade 2 pyrexia (temperature 38.9 C) and grade 1 chills within the first 2 weeks of dabrafenib/trametinib, managed with dabrafenib dose reduction to 100 mg twice daily and scheduled acetaminophen.
At age 38, patient developed grade 1 nausea and grade 1 rash attributed to trametinib, managed with ondansetron and topical corticosteroids.
At age 39, surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at 6 months showed no evidence of disease recurrence.
At age 39, LDH remained normal at 152 U/L with no clinical evidence of disease recurrence and patient continued adjuvant dabrafenib/trametinib at reduced dose.
At age 39, 12-month surveillance PET/CT was negative for recurrent or metastatic melanoma.
At age 39, patient completed 12 months of adjuvant dabrafenib/trametinib with manageable toxicity and no evidence of recurrence.
At age 40, surveillance CT at 6 months post-treatment completion showed no evidence of recurrence.
At age 40, MRI brain surveillance remained negative for intracranial disease.

At age 40, patient remained disease-free at 24 months from initial diagnosis, with LDH stable at 139 U/L, continuing routine surveillance with clinical examinations every 3 months and imaging every 6 months.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 38, shave biopsy followed by excisional biopsy of the right forearm lesion revealed superficial spreading melanoma with Breslow thickness of 1.8 mm, Clark level IV, ulceration present, mitotic rate 3/mm2, no regression, no microsatellites, and negative deep margin on excision.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 38, sentinel lymph node pathology showed isolated tumor cells to micrometastasis (0.4 mm) in 1 of 2 nodes, S100 and Melan-A positive, no extracapsular extension.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 38, next-generation sequencing of the primary melanoma identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 35%), NRAS wild-type, no TERT promoter mutation, wild-type TP53, and tumor mutational burden of 8 mutations per megabase.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 38, baseline PET/CT showed no evidence of distant metastatic disease.

Please correlate clinically.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 38, MRI brain with gadolinium contrast was negative for intracranial metastases.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 38, LDH was normal at 145 U/L, and multidisciplinary tumor board recommended adjuvant systemic therapy given sentinel node positivity.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Stage III/IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation. Treated with combination BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib/trametinib) and/or checkpoint immunotherapy (nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab). Some with brain metastases. Include LDH levels.
